header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Organizational Development professional who thrives on designing training programs and managing learning operations, partnering with business teams to identify development needs, and measuring learning effectiveness through data. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve people development—through human-centered, systematic approaches.

bullets.text: Upskill HR team in {data|data analytics} to audit 300+ leave cases, successfully refuting cost assumptions via {data|evidence-based insights}
Empower HR to present executive-ready insights on $362K in leave costs, leading to policy redesigns and first aid training
Conduct needs assessments with 25+ global {cross|stakeholders} to identify learning gaps and design 3 new educational {prog|programs}
Develop guides and workflows for 70+ {cross|cross-functional} staff, improving {prog|program} knowledge and reducing onboarding time by 75%
Facilitate training webinars for 600+ participants, enhancing {prog|program understanding} and driving a 78% client conversion rate
Collaborate with 4 teams and 8 vendors to launch 15+ educational {prog|programs}; drive continuous improvement via {data|feedback analysis}
Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|feedback data} into actionable reports that informed engagement strategies
Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences to amplify employee voice and guide C-suite decisions on satisfaction drivers
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal training on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Improved {ex|employee experience} via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects focusing on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
Partnered with HRBP team to design and pilot an Inclusive Leadership workshop for senior leaders, later scaled to 23 global locations
Facilitated smooth organizational change by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing {cross|cross-functional stakeholders}
Streamlined onboarding processes across 3 global locations by conducting gap analyses and clarifying job responsibilities
Delivered strategic HR solutions for business priorities across projects in employee relations, headcount, and succession planning
Developed proficiency across various HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management
Filled a challenging international role by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and researching global compensation details
Engaged global talent via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months

jd.meta: Sandvik Inc — HR Specialist / Training & Development, Alachua FL

changeLog: 2026-05-25: Tailored for Sandvik HR/T&D Specialist. L&D + HR hybrid role type. InGenius title: Program Enablement Manager. Bullet Set A (L&D) with ODN Pair A. Vestas: partnered/piloted variant + HR strategic variant (headcount, succession planning) to signal HRBP depth. SAP added to HRIS skills (Sandvik is a global industrial company likely using SAP). Summary emphasizes T&D operations and HR partnership — matches Sandvik's combined HR/T&D scope.
